name: Hillary Clinton
subject: In a word? No.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
My answer was no. | Friend --

Today during an interview, I was asked if I think Donald Trump is qualified to be president.

My answer was no.

This week alone, he’s alienated Great Britain (one of our closest allies) and praised the reckless dictator in North Korea. A qualified nominee would recognize that either of those statements is a colossal mistake.

But a qualified nominee would also never suggest that we ban all Muslims from entering our country, violating our deeply-held principle of freedom of religion and endangering our partnerships with allies who are absolutely essential in the fight against terrorism.

So, no -- Donald Trump is not qualified to be president.

But if we don’t work as hard as we can every day from now until November 8th, he’ll be the next president of the United States.

**The world is counting on us to win. I know you won’t let me down -- chip in to say you’re with me and ready to take this fight all the way to the White House (we’ll send you a free sticker to say thank you):** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjU1MzYzMjUS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODAzMDQ1OTMzS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Hillary

name: staff@maggiehassan.com
subject: Link of the Week: Donald Trump vs. Root canals
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
_This is our Link of the Week email -- the things that we're sharing around the office, and we think everyone on Team Maggie should know about. We hope you'll enjoy clicking around as much as we do._
Hey there,
Have you seen the new poll from Public Policy Polling about Kelly Ayotte's preferred presidential candidate, Donald Trump?
It's pretty revealing. Here's one of the questions:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd3dd/2a380d31/13ee9bc5/f8b4b9/999769269/VEsH/ "http://www.publicpolicypolling.com/pdf/2015/PPP_Release_National_51016.pdf")
Unlike Ayotte, most of those surveyed think more highly of a root canal than they do the GOP nominee. Ouch, that hurts worse than oral surgery.
There are some other fun things in here about the Donald (he's also less popular than lice) -- we think it's a hoot, [**check it out.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd3dd/2a380d31/13ee9bc5/f8b4b9/999769269/VEsE/ "http://www.publicpolicypolling.com/pdf/2015/PPP_Release_National_51016.pdf")
-- Team Maggie
P.S. -- Unfortunately, root canals and lice are a fact of life. Luckily, the Trump/Ayotte agenda doesn't have to be. name: Rob Portman (personal)
subject: More than any candidate in the country
body:
| Peter --

**Out-of-state, liberal special interest groups have reserved $20 million to support Ted Strickland this fall -- more than any Senate candidate in the country!** Senator Harry Reid's SuperPAC already spent $2 million on misleading attack ads and they just reserved another $9.5 million in TV ads for the fall. Peter, the National Democrats have made this Senate seat their #1 target. I am committed to winning this race and continuing to fight for a brighter future for Ohio with more jobs and better wages. But I must ask for your help to fight back. Would you be willing to donate to the Grassroots Victory fund? These funds are used to fund our field offices, knock doors, and make phone calls. Over the past year, we’ve contacted nearly 2 million targeted voters, spreading our message of fighting for a brighter future for Ohio. subject: Oklahoma just voted to make abortion a criminal offense
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Sending doctors to prison? | Friend --

Breaking news -- the Oklahoma legislature just passed a bill that would make it a criminal offense for a doctor to perform an abortion, punishable by up to three years in prison.

The legislation will be challenged in court, but Donald Trump has announced that if he wins the presidency, he’ll appoint Supreme Court justices who’d overturn _Roe v. Wade_ \-- which could allow laws like these to stand all over the country. Our future could be extremists trying to take away women’s fundamental rights and putting doctors in jail for giving us basic health care.

It’s going to take a smart campaign with skilled organizers and targeted messaging to make sure voters know exactly what’s at stake in this election -- that’s what we’re building right now. But if we want to beat Trump, all of us need to step up.

**Can we count on you to chip in $1 today? It’s never been more important to make sure that Hillary is our next president:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjU1NDI2MzcS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODAzMTU2NzUyS0&mt=1&rt=0)

| |

**It's official! I'm on the ballot!** Yesterday, I officially filed to run for Governor! My wonderful wife Barbara and my parents joined me at the State Capitol. They've been supporting my campaign since I announced just over a year ago and I'm so thankful to have their help.

It could be easy to understate the significance of filing my candidacy because of all that we've done so far - we've spent the last year talking to voters and community leaders in every county and we'll continue to do so through November. But this was an important moment of the campaign and I'm so grateful to have been able to share it with my family, without whom I couldn't hope to have made it this far.

Thank you Barbara, thank you Mom and Dad, and thank you to all the Washingtonians who have put their faith and support in this campaign. With your help I know we'll win this November and bring true reform and leadership to Olympia.

Bill Bryant

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Our political revolution, powered by you
body:
#
When we started this campaign, my greatest fear was that if we did not do well that it would be a setback not just for me, but for the ideas driving our campaign.
We had no campaign organization, no money, and very little name recognition. The corporate media called us "fringe," we were taking on the entire Democratic establishment, and we were down 60 points or more in the national polls.
Then a supporter from Chicago, Illinois made the first contribution to our campaign -- for $3. A few minutes later, $50 from a supporter in California, then $10 from someone in Georgia. A little more than 12 months later, **I am humbled to share that our campaign has received more than 7.6 million contributions through April** , more than any presidential candidate at this point in a campaign ever. And we're just getting started.
Your support has powered us to 21 victories and a much larger lead against Donald Trump than Secretary Clinton's campaign. So we've created a website to show everyone the depth and diversity of our political revolution. **[It's very important that you visit and share it with everyone you know today](https://berniesanders.com/revolution/ "https://berniesanders.com/revolution/")**.
\- Bernie

name: =?utf-8?Q?Team=20Scott?=
subject: Your Campaign Update
body:
| | | _Week in Action_
---
[View this email in your browser](http://us13.campaign-archive1.com/?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1ab7a0d136&e=19cebfe173)
---
| |
---
| Greetings,
---
| Phil officially kicked off his campaign for Governor with his _Green Flag Tour_ making its first stop this week. Read more about the launch of the tour and Team Scott’s other efforts over the last week, below.

**IT’S TIME FOR A LEADER WHO WILL MAKE THE ECONOMY A TOP PRIORITY**
Phil spoke with the _Caledonian Record_ this week to share his vision for Vermont and how, as Governor, he’ll make certain the economy is the top priority for legislators.

As Phil told the _Record_ , Vermonters are “struggling to make ends meet and are working two and three jobs to do it.” Yet, as he points out, of more than 1,200 bills introduced in the previous biennium, very few were focused on improving the economy. We made no meaningful progress on the economy this year.

Phil expands on his proposals to set a 90-day limit to the legislative session in order to focus the Legislature on bills that will improve the economy and reduce costs, as well as his ideas for improving education and other top issues facing Vermonters. Click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e6869d9003&e=19cebfe173) to view the full interview.
*******
**TEAM SCOTT HITS THE ROAD**
Phil has always made it a priority to meet with Vermonters around the state while serving as Lt. Governor and working as a small business owner. Now he’s kicking the campaign season into high gear with a statewide _Green Flag Tour_ , with stops in all of Vermont’s 14 counties.

Team Scott hit the road this week in “Blizzard” – our mobile campaign office – to hear ideas and perspectives directly from Vermonters. As Phil said, “Listening and learning is the key to bringing people together around our goals of a stronger, more affordable Vermont and restoring faith and trust in state government.”

Find out when Blizzard will roll into your area, and see a full listing of the _Green Flag Tour_ and Team Scott events, [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7b512794d6&e=19cebfe173).

Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT ***
**THANK A VET MOTORCYCLE RIDE ON MAY 21**
Tomorrow Phil will join hundreds of Vermonters from around the state in support of veterans’ charities, and we hope to see each and every one of you there. The Thank a Vet Motorcycle Ride will kick off from locations in Rutland, St. Albans, St. Johnsbury and White River Junction at 10 a.m. on Saturday, May 21.

Riders will converge in downtown Barre at 12 p.m. where a ceremony honoring Vermont veterans and an afternoon barbecue will be open to all. Funds raised at the barbecue will benefit the Josh Pallotta Fund, dedicated to helping veterans with PTSD, and the Friends of Veterans Organization, which helps veterans to avoid homelessness.

Supporting our military and veterans is something we can and should all commit to – we owe them so much. For more information visit the [event’s Facebook page](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=04b6f06b25&e=19cebfe173). We hope to see you there.

**AN IMPORTANT DISCUSSION ON OUR OPIOID CRISIS**
A heartfelt thank you to Dartmouth-Hitchcock and _VT Digger_ for organizing an informative panel discussion on Vermont’s opioid crisis. Phil attended the Digger Dialogue in White River Junction on Wednesday. It’s important that we understand how this issue affects us all. It is a crisis that touches every pocket of our state – no community is immune. Read more about the discussion at [VT Digger](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=09d185d3de&e=19cebfe173).

Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT
**LISTENING & LEARNING AROUND VERMONT: A WEEK IN PHOTOS**
Check out photos from some of this week’s highlights, where Phil participated in forums at **the Association of Vermont Credit Unions** ’ annual conference and with the **Hunger Council of Vermont** ; stopped by the **Devils Bowl Car Show** , **Rutland Red Knights** ’ blessing of the bikes, and the **Vermont Fuel Dealers’** annual meeting; and met with Vermonters in **Burlington** , **Charlotte** , **St. Jay** and **Windsor**.

Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT

Next week Phil will be at the [Vermont Futures Tour](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ee951d7ed3&e=19cebfe173), the [Vermont Sheriff’s and Chief of Police annual meeting](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=6718c43265&e=19cebfe173) and the [Essex Memorial Day Parade](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=54ef4f9b3f&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

_Phil: We have a crisis of affordability_
“With all the fees and taxes, and so forth, affordability is a real crisis in this state...the answer is growing the population and stemming the flow of workers out-of-state.” (Source: WCAX: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=08c80e8a9e&e=19cebfe173))

_Phil: Honor our fallen police officers_
“When you keep us safe and run towards a problem instead of away from a problem, that separates those that serve in a uniform from the rest of us." (Source: Fox44: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=6acdfa3068&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

_Burlington Free Press: Shelburne government finds youth movement_
The _Burlington Free Press_ highlights a unique Shelburne program, working to foster interest in public service and civic responsibility amongst its youth. Phil believes it is vital to get our youth involved and engaged with local and state government. We need more regular, every day Vermonters to take interest and run for public office to ensure our Legislature reflects our values. Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=65908bdb70&e=19cebfe173). Click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9c8ccf3eb8&e=19cebfe173) to learn more about Phil’s legislative proposal that would, in part, help build a Citizen Legislature.

Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=33c315d4e3&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4cb783d944&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,

Team Scott

Supporting our military and veterans is something we can and should all commit to – we owe them so much. For more information visit the . We hope to see you there. AN IMPORTANT DISCUSSION ON OUR OPIOID CRISIS A heartfelt thank you to Dartmouth-Hitchcock and VT Digger for organizing an informative panel discussion on Vermont’s opioid crisis. Phil attended the Digger Dialogue in White River Junction on Wednesday. It’s important that we understand how this issue affects us all. It is a crisis that touches every pocket of our state – no community is immune. Read more about the discussion at . Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT LISTENING & LEARNING AROUND VERMONT: A WEEK IN PHOTOS Check out photos from some of this week’s highlights, where Phil participated in forums at the Association of Vermont Credit Unions ’ annual conference and with the Hunger Council of Vermont ; stopped by the Devils Bowl Car Show , Rutland Red Knights ’ blessing of the bikes, and the Vermont Fuel Dealers’ annual meeting; and met with Vermonters in Burlington , Charlotte , St. Jay and Windsor. Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT Next week Phil will be at the , the and the . ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS Phil: We have a crisis of affordability “With all the fees and taxes, and so forth, affordability is a real crisis in this state...the answer is growing the population and stemming the flow of workers out-of-state.” (Source: WCAX: ) Phil: Honor our fallen police officers “When you keep us safe and run towards a problem instead of away from a problem, that separates those that serve in a uniform from the rest of us." (Source: Fox44: ) ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS** Burlington Free Press: Shelburne government finds youth movement The Burlington Free Press highlights a unique Shelburne program, working to foster interest in public service and civic responsibility amongst its youth. Phil believes it is vital to get our youth involved and engaged with local and state government. We need more regular, every day Vermonters to take interest and run for public office to ensure our Legislature reflects our values. Read the full article . Click to learn more about Phil’s legislative proposal that would, in part, help build a Citizen Legislature. Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – and . name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: Tell Senate GOPs: Do Your Job or No Summer Vacation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
|

>
> Friend,
>
> Over the last few months, it's been shocking to see how unapologetically Mitch McConnell and other Republicans have declared their intention not to consider the President's nominee for the Supreme Court.
>
> Frankly, it is unacceptable. That's why I, along with a group of my colleagues, just introduced legislation requiring Congress to remain in session through the summer recess unless the Senate holds confirmation hearings for a nominee by July 19th.
>
> **[Please add your name now to tell Republicans in Congress: There will be no summer vacation until you do your jobs!](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91d1f/70cdc2ba/47ef21d2/396d8b2/4022361020/VEsH/ "http://go.democrataction.com/page/s/scotus-act?source=cvh&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Please+add+your+name+now+to+tell+Republi&utm_campaign=em052016")**
>
> July 19th will mark 125 days since the President nominated U.S. Circuit Judge Merrick B. Garland for the Supreme Court -- which would be the longest period a Supreme Court nominee has ever had to wait for a hearing in U.S. history.
>
> **Here are the facts:** Republicans in the Senate know that Judge Garland is a qualified candidate for Supreme Court, but they're continuing to play politics with the highest court in the land.
>
> That's not a decision we can afford. And not the type of service to our country that Americans deserve. We must come together and make our government work for all its citizens. But if we don't make our voices heard today, nothing will change.
>
> **[Sign on today and tell Republicans in the Senate: Do Your Job!](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91d1f/70cdc2ba/47ef21d2/396d8b3/4022361020/VEsE/ "http://go.democrataction.com/page/s/scotus-act?source=cvh&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Sign+on+today+and+tell+Republicans+in+th&utm_campaign=em052016")**
>
> [**http://action.vanhollen.org/DoYourJob**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91d1f/70cdc2ba/47ef21d2/396d8b0/4022361020/VEsF/ "http://go.democrataction.com/page/s/scotus-act?source=cvh&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=3+-+httpactionvanhollenorgDoYourJob&utm_campaign=em052016")
>
> Thank you for speaking out on this critical issue,
>
> Chris
---
